philosophy.statement: I treat the frontend as a systems problem, not a coat of paint. I design the architecture first — clear boundaries, a token-driven design system, a deliberate server/client split — so interfaces stay fast, accessible, and easy to change as products grow. What matters is the decision behind each pixel: what to build, what to leave out, and why.

ai.title: Engineering with AI, deliberately
ai.intro: AI accelerates the work; the engineering judgment stays human. Documentation drives the process, decisions are made deliberately, and every change is reviewed and verified before it ships.

documentation.title: Documentation-driven
documentation.description: Intent is written down before code — the spec drives the build, not the other way around.

decisions.title: Deliberate decisions

verification.title: Reviewed & verified
verification.description: Every change is reviewed and verified against the performance budget before it ships.

meta.title: About
meta.description: Frontend engineer building scalable, production-grade web applications — with the architecture, performance, and accessibility of a modern AI SaaS product.

intro.headline: I engineer premium, scalable frontends for AI products.
intro.lead: I design and build production-grade web applications — owning architecture, performance, and accessibility from the first commit to deployment.
narrative: I'm a frontend engineer who thinks in systems, not screens. I care about the parts a user never sees — the component boundaries, the state model, the render path — because those decide whether a product stays fast and maintainable as it grows.

My background is in Artificial Intelligence, so I'm fluent in the data-heavy dashboards and AI-driven interfaces I build, and comfortable with the real workflows behind them. Right now I'm going deeper into Next.js, backend fundamentals, and AI systems.
Frontend is where engineering meets the person using it. Getting it right means performance, accessibility, and clarity are treated as requirements — never as polish applied at the end.
intro.availability: Open to frontend & AI product roles

philosophy.title: How I engineer
philosophy.intro: The convictions that shape every technical decision I make.
philosophy.statement: I don't write UI code — I own the system behind the interface. Every choice is measured against one question: will this keep the product fast, accessible, and easy to change a year from now?

performance.title: Performance first
performance.description: Speed is a feature. I measure, profile, and budget it — Core Web Vitals and bundle size are constraints I design within, never afterthoughts.

accessibility.title: Accessibility by default
accessibility.description: WCAG AA is the baseline. Semantic HTML, keyboard paths, and focus management are built in from the first commit, not retrofitted.

designSystems.title: Design systems
designSystems.description: I build with tokens and composable components, so an interface stays visually consistent and a change lands in one place, not fifty.

architecture.title: Scalable architecture
architecture.description: Clear boundaries, feature-based structure, and disciplined state — designed to survive a growing codebase and a growing team.

maintainability.title: Maintainability & DX
maintainability.description: Code is read far more than it's written. I optimize for the next engineer: typed, documented, and simple to reason about.

ownership.title: Ownership
ownership.description: I take responsibility for outcomes, not tickets — from architecture through deployment, monitoring, and the follow-up.

process.title: How I turn ambiguity into shipped software
process.intro: A repeatable workflow that keeps quality predictable — from the first conversation to production.

understand.title: Understand
understand.description: Clarify the problem, the users, and the constraints before touching code — the real requirement is rarely the first one stated.

research.title: Research
research.description: Survey approaches, prior art, and trade-offs, so the decision is driven by evidence rather than habit.

architect.title: Architect
architect.description: Design the data flow, component boundaries, and state model up front — structure before implementation.

build.title: Build
build.description: Implement in small, reviewable increments with types and tests, keeping the main branch always shippable.

optimize.title: Optimize
optimize.description: Profile and tune rendering, bundle size, and Core Web Vitals — measured against real budgets, not guesses.

review.title: Review
review.description: Self-review, verify accessibility and edge cases, and invite critique before anything ships.

deliver.title: Deliver
deliver.description: Ship with confidence, watch it in production, and fold what I learn back into the next iteration.

curiosity.title: Curiosity
curiosity.description: I ask why until I understand the system, not just the symptom — every project is a chance to learn something I didn't know.

quality.title: Quality

ownership.title: Ownership
ownership.description: I own outcomes end to end, and I don't consider something done until it works in production.

meta.title: Engineering
meta.description: The engineering behind the portfolio — how I think about architecture, performance, accessibility, and the decisions and workflow that ship it, with the living docs that back each one.

intro.title: I engineer systems, not just screens.
intro.lead: What matters to me is the system behind the interface — how it's structured, how it performs, and how it stays maintainable as it grows. Here's how I think about architecture, performance, accessibility, and the decisions behind them — each backed by the living docs that govern this codebase.

problemSolving.title: Problem solving
problemSolving.description: I start from the real constraint, not the first feature — the hard part is usually understanding the problem, not typing the code.

maintainability.title: Maintainability
maintainability.description: Code is read far more than it's written; I optimize for the engineer who touches it next.

performance.title: Performance
performance.description: Speed is a budget I design within — measured against Core Web Vitals, never assumed.

ux.title: User experience
ux.description: The interface is where engineering meets a person — accessibility and clarity are requirements, not polish.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.description: I design boundaries and data flow before implementation, so the structure survives growth and a bigger team.

architecture.title: How I approach architecture
architecture.intro: Architecture is a sequence of deliberate decisions, not a diagram drawn once. This is the loop I run for any non-trivial system.

understand.title: Understand requirements
understand.description: Pin down the real problem, the users, and the constraints before any structure is drawn.

modularize.title: Break into modules
modularize.description: Decompose the system into cohesive units with clear responsibilities and boundaries.

reusable.title: Design reusable systems
reusable.description: Turn recurring patterns into composable components and primitives, not one-off code.

scalability.title: Think about scalability
scalability.description: Choose structures that hold up as features, data, and the team grow.

optimize.title: Optimize performance
optimize.description: Design the render and data path for speed from the start, then measure it.

review.title: Review
review.description: Check the design against edge cases, accessibility, and the trade-offs it accepts.

iterate.title: Iterate
iterate.description: Fold what production teaches back into the architecture — it's never finished at v1.

build.title: How I build frontend systems
build.intro: The pipeline every change runs, from an open question to something live in production.

research.title: Research
research.description: Survey approaches, prior art, and trade-offs so the decision is informed, not habitual.

planning.title: Planning
planning.description: Define scope, the definition of done, and the risks before writing code.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.description: Design the data flow, component boundaries, and state model up front.

implementation.title: Implementation
implementation.description: Build in small, typed, reviewable increments; keep the main branch shippable.

optimization.title: Optimization
optimization.description: Profile and tune rendering, bundle size, and Core Web Vitals against real budgets.

accessibility.title: Accessibility
accessibility.description: Verify semantics, keyboard paths, focus, and reduced motion — part of done, not after.

testing.title: Testing
testing.description: Cover the logic and the edge cases that matter, and guard against regressions.

deployment.title: Deployment
deployment.description: Ship with confidence, watch it in production, and feed what I learn back in.

performance.title: Performance is a feature, not a final pass
performance.intro: Speed is designed in and measured, never bolted on. A few of the levers I reach for.
performance.statement: I budget performance the way I budget scope: Core Web Vitals are constraints I design within, and every kilobyte of client JavaScript has to justify itself.

rendering.title: Rendering strategy
rendering.description: Server-render by default; pick SSG, SSR, or ISR per route by what the content actually needs.

hydration.title: Hydration cost
hydration.description: Keep interactivity at the leaves — less client JavaScript means less to parse, hydrate, and run.

streaming.title: Streaming
streaming.description: Stream server-rendered HTML with Suspense so content paints progressively, not all-or-nothing.

codeSplitting.title: Code splitting
codeSplitting.description: Load what a route needs and defer the rest, so the first paint stays lean.

images.title: Image optimization
images.description: Right-sized, modern formats with explicit dimensions — fast to load and zero layout shift.

caching.title: Caching
caching.description: Cache aggressively at build time and the edge, so repeat work is never paid twice.

vitals.title: Core Web Vitals
vitals.description: LCP, CLS, and INP are tracked as targets, not vanity numbers.

bundle.title: Bundle discipline
bundle.description: Watch dependency weight — a small feature should never ship a large library.

accessibility.title: Accessible by default, not on request
accessibility.intro: WCAG AA is the baseline every interface starts from. Here's what that means in practice.
accessibility.statement: Accessibility isn't a feature added for some users — it's the difference between software that works for everyone and software that quietly excludes people.

semantics.title: Semantic HTML
semantics.description: The right element first — real headings, lists, buttons, and landmarks carry meaning for free.

keyboard.title: Keyboard support
keyboard.description: Every interaction works without a mouse, with a visible, logical focus order.

aria.title: ARIA, sparingly
aria.description: ARIA fills the gaps semantics can't — used precisely, never as a substitute for the right element.

reducedMotion.title: Reduced motion
reducedMotion.description: Every animation has a calm, honored prefers-reduced-motion fallback.

contrast.title: Contrast
contrast.description: Text and UI meet AA contrast in both themes — verified, not eyeballed.

screenReaders.title: Screen readers
screenReaders.description: Content reads in a sensible order, with meaningful names and live updates where needed.

designSystems.title: Building UI that scales
designSystems.intro: A design system is what keeps a growing interface consistent and cheap to change. This page is built on one.
designSystems.statement: Every color, space, and type value on this site resolves to a token — a hardcoded value is a lintable error, not a style choice.

tokens.title: Tokens
tokens.description: One source of truth for color, space, type, and motion — themeable and enforceable in CI.

components.title: Components
components.description: A small set of well-defined primitives that everything else is built from.

variants.title: Variants
variants.description: Controlled options over one-off overrides, so a component stays predictable.

composition.title: Composition
composition.description: Small pieces combined, not deep inheritance — flexibility without complexity.

consistency.title: Consistency
consistency.description: The same spacing, motion, and states everywhere, because they come from the same source.

maintainability.title: Maintainability
maintainability.description: A change lands in one place and propagates — not fifty find-and-replaces.

ai.title: AI accelerates the work; judgment stays human
ai.intro: I use AI tooling daily — deliberately, and with a human owning every decision.
ai.statement: AI makes me faster at the mechanical parts. It doesn't decide the architecture, own the trade-offs, or approve the code — that's engineering, and it stays mine.

architecture.title: AI-assisted architecture
architecture.description: I use AI to pressure-test designs and explore alternatives — then I make the call.

review.title: AI code review
review.description: A first-pass reviewer for bugs and edge cases, before a human review — never instead of one.

docsFirst.title: Documentation-first
docsFirst.description: The governing decision is written before the code, so the AI and I work from the same intent.

validation.title: Human validation
validation.description: Nothing ships unread — every AI-generated line is understood, tested, and owned.

tools.title: The tools I reach for
tools.intro: Grouped by the job they do — chosen deliberately, not collected.

principles.title: The non-negotiables
principles.intro: The convictions this codebase actually enforces — most are checkable in CI, not just aspirations.

performance.title: Performance first
performance.description: Speed is a requirement with a budget, measured on every build.

accessibility.title: Accessibility by default
accessibility.description: WCAG AA from the first commit, for everyone.

serverFirst.title: Server first
serverFirst.description: Render on the server by default; ship the client only what must be interactive.

maintainability.title: Maintainability
maintainability.description: Optimize for the next engineer — typed, documented, simple.

scalability.title: Scalability
scalability.description: Structure that holds as the codebase and the team grow.

ownership.title: Ownership
ownership.description: Responsible for outcomes end to end, from the decision to production.

documentation.title: Documentation
documentation.description: The decision is written before the code — intent is explicit and durable.

learning.title: Continuous learning
learning.description: The stack keeps moving; so do I.

decisions.title: Engineering decisions
decisions.intro: A few of the architecture decisions behind this site — the context, the choice, and what it cost. The full ADR log goes deeper.

appRouter.title: Why the App Router
appRouter.explanation: Server-rendered, localized HTML and top-tier performance were the priorities, with no runtime backend.
appRouter.tradeoff: A real learning curve at the server/client boundary, and deliberate coupling to Next.js.

serverComponents.title: Why Server Components
serverComponents.explanation: Rendering on the server by default ships minimal client JavaScript and maximal edge-deliverable HTML.
serverComponents.tradeoff: Engineers must consciously manage the server/client boundary instead of reaching for client state freely.

tokens.title: Why design tokens
tokens.explanation: Every design value resolves to a token, so consistency is structural and enforced in CI.
tokens.tradeoff: Utility-dense markup and a Tailwind theme that must stay in lockstep with the tokens.

mdx.title: Why MDX for content
mdx.explanation: Projects, docs, and journal entries are schema-validated MDX — content as data, not hardcoded markup.
mdx.tradeoff: A small content pipeline to maintain, and two language trees to keep in sync.

featureFirst.title: Why feature-first architecture
featureFirst.explanation: Code is organized by feature with inward-only dependencies, so features stay cohesive as the app grows.
featureFirst.tradeoff: Requires real judgment about what is genuinely shared versus feature-specific.

deepDives.title: The engineering docs
deepDives.intro: The living documentation that governs this codebase — the same docs the code is held to, made public.

journal.title: Development journal
journal.intro: The build, milestone by milestone — the decisions and turning points behind the portfolio.

cta.title: Care about how it's built?
cta.subtitle: If you're building something where architecture, performance, and craft matter, I'd love to talk through the hard parts.
